_meta.description: Quarterly merchant AI-silicon revenue per supplier in $M. Calendar quarters mapped from each issuer's fiscal calendar (NVDA FY ends late Jan; AMD FY = calendar; AVGO FY ends early Nov — AVGO Q4 FY25 = Aug-Oct 2025 = calendar 2025Q3). Pulled from C:\Users\longr\Project\equityreport\_official_packs\{NVDA,AMD,AVGO}.
_meta.convention: amounts are USD millions. NVDA = Data Center segment revenue (compute+networking). AMD = Data Center segment revenue (Instinct GPUs + EPYC server CPUs). AVGO = 'AI semiconductor revenue' as called out in earnings press releases (custom AI accelerators + AI networking silicon).
_meta.update_cadence: after each issuer's earnings release (quarterly, staggered)
_meta.addresses_google_amzn_asic_question: Google TPU and AWS Trainium revenue is not separately disclosed by GOOGL/AMZN — but AVGO designs custom silicon and AI networking for hyperscalers (TPU networking) and is the closest available proxy.

AVGO.note: derived from AVGO Q4 FY25 PR stating +74% YoY: 8200 / 1.74
derived from AVGO Q1 FY26 PR stating +106% YoY: 8400 / 2.06
AVGO Q4 FY25 covers Aug–Oct 2025 ≈ calendar 2025Q3
AVGO.source: AVGO_8-K_20251211_Ex99.1_Q4FY2025_PR ('$8.2 billion ... custom AI accelerators and Ethernet AI switches')

AVGO.note: AVGO Q1 FY26 covers Nov 2025–Jan 2026 ≈ calendar 2025Q4
AVGO.source: AVGO_8-K_20260304_Ex99.1_Q1FY2026_PR ('AI revenue of $8.4 billion grew 106% year-over-year')
